text.tspan: A: To transform a permutation p into its next
1. Find pivot (largest index i of p such that
p[i] < p[i+1]). If there is no pivot
2. Find successor (largest index i after pivot
pivot = 2 since 2 is the largest i such that
p[i] < p[i + 1], in this case p[2] < p[3] → 1<7
successor = 4 since 4 is the largest i such that
p[i] > p[pivot], in this case p[4] > p[2] → 5>1
Swapping p[pivot] and p[successor] results in
This is the next iteration after the original p.
A: Since nums1 and nums2 are sorted and nums1 has
as many empty elements as numbers are in nums2.
We can merge them both by traversing them from
right to left filling the empty elements in nums1
with the largest numbers from nums1 or nums2
For this we need help from 3 pointers:
- i to traverse the numbers in nums1.
- j to traverse the numbers in nums2.
- k to populate nums1 with numbers starting
from the last position of nums1 (to fill the
While j >= 0 (meaning there are still numbers in
nums2 that have not been merged) compare nums1[i]
with nums2[j]. If i >= 0 and nums1[i] > nums2[j]
copy nums1[i] into nums1[k] and substract 1 from
i and k. Otherwise, copy nums2[j] into nums1[k]
and substract 1 from j and k.
When this process is finished, nums1 and nums2
will have been merged into nums1.
The following is a Python implementation.
This is how the algorithm moves values between
arrays and how the pointers change in each
At this point, nums1 and nums2 have been merged.
